search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng253 fever suspected sepsis assessment observations risk criteria
site:dha.gov.ae fever children Issue 2 virtual assessment referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic sepsis diagnosis or risk tier
antibiotic, antipyretic, fluid and admission recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Fever alone does not establish sepsis or its cause.
No medicine, dose, treatment, investigation or disposition is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Broad fever workflow spanning adults and children; NICE sepsis evidence applies when sepsis is suspected and DHA evidence applies to pediatric fever, leaving many non-sepsis adult presentations incompletely covered.
setting_applicability: Emergency assessment; DHA virtual pediatric evidence only partially transfers to in-person ED care.
UAE_applicability: DHA pediatric evidence is directly local; NICE assessment domains require UAE sepsis, infection, antimicrobial, laboratory and emergency pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG253 (2026) and DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG253 and the registered DHA source were current in the reviewed catalogues.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE suspected-sepsis recognition section supports documenting onset, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician risk context when infection or sepsis is clinically considered; it does not equate fever with sepsis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact assessment section supports measured vital data, focused assessment, illness severity and general appearance only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact risk-criteria section supports recording time-critical symptoms or abnormal observations only when clinician-assessed; it does not generate a risk classification.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA pediatric fever assessment section independently supports severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and appearance when assessing a child; it is not generalized to adults.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact pediatric referral section supports documenting escalation or handover only when clinician-decided for a child.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age-specific fever thresholds, source symptoms, immunocompromise, travel/exposure, examination, tests, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
